description_long: This object is a cluster of four large, irregularly shaped rocks with rough, weathered surfaces and natural, angular contours. The rocks are closely packed together, forming a low mound with visible crevices and overlapping edges. Their coloration is predominantly grayish-brown with patches of lighter and darker tones, suggesting exposure to the elements and possible lichen or mineral deposits. The surfaces are uneven, with some faces appearing flatter and others more jagged, contributing to a rugged, natural appearance. The rocks are solid and heavy, with no visible artificial modifications or markings. This formation would serve as a substantial physical barrier or naturalistic feature in an urban environment.
description: A cluster of four large, rough, grayish-brown rocks with irregular, weathered surfaces and natural angular shapes, closely packed together.
description_view_0: The front view shows the cluster with three prominent rocks in the foreground and one partially visible behind, highlighting the varied heights and overlapping arrangement.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the angular edges and the way the rocks lean against each other, with two main rocks dominating the profile.
description_view_2: From the rear, the rocks appear more tightly grouped, with the largest rock's broad surface facing outward and the others partially obscured.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the irregular outlines and the crevices between the rocks, with the tallest rock on the left and a more open gap visible in the center.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
